assertion_results.text: Tracing configuration is generated for all 3 services (api-gateway, order-service, inventory-service) with service-specific names
assertion_results.evidence: Three configuration files generated with service names set. However, all three use Node.js configuration despite inventory-service being Python.

assertion_results.text: W3C Trace Context headers (traceparent) are propagated between services on outgoing HTTP calls
assertion_results.evidence: Custom X-Request-ID header is passed between services but not W3C traceparent format. Header value is a UUID, not the W3C trace context format (version-trace_id-parent_id-trace_flags).

assertion_results.text: A correlation ID middleware extracts trace ID from incoming requests or generates a new one if absent
assertion_results.evidence: Middleware generates UUID and attaches to request as X-Request-ID. Extracts from incoming header if present.

assertion_results.text: Correlation IDs are injected into log output so logs can be correlated with traces
assertion_results.evidence: Request ID is attached to req object but not injected into logger context. Logs use console.log without correlation ID.

assertion_results.text: Child spans are created for outgoing HTTP calls from api-gateway to order-service and from order-service to inventory-service
assertion_results.evidence: No span creation. No OpenTelemetry SDK used. Tracing is implemented as simple request ID passing, not proper distributed tracing with spans.

assertion_results.text: Both Node.js (api-gateway, order-service) and Python (inventory-service) configurations are provided with language-appropriate OpenTelemetry SDKs
assertion_results.evidence: All three services use Node.js configuration. inventory-service should use Python OpenTelemetry SDK but was given a Node.js config file.

assertion_results.text: Trace exporter is configured to send to a Jaeger-compatible OTLP endpoint (configurable via environment variable)
assertion_results.evidence: No trace exporter configured. No OpenTelemetry SDK used. Request IDs are only passed via HTTP headers, not exported to any tracing backend.
